mentions.raw_name: The innkeep of the Merchant's House
The ship's cook on the Meadowlark
The book Maester Kedry gave Gerris
Windblown sellswords/recruiters (unnamed, four at the table plus singers)
Daenerys Targaryen ("the silver queen," "the dragon queen," "the most beautiful woman in the world")
The Sand Snakes (referenced as a group)
Nymeria (historical; burned her ten thousand ships)
The Tattered Prince (leader of the Windblown, referenced)
Ship's cook on the Meadowlark (unnamed, recalled)
A triarch of Volantis (unnamed, seen atop elephant)
The Windblown (sellsword company, recruiting in Volantis)
The Long Lances (sellsword company, already shipped to Yunkai)
The Company of the Cat (sellsword company, filling ranks)
The Unsullied (spearmen seen guarding the triarch)
Foreign gods (temples along the Volantis waterfront where sailors sacrifice)
"If the gods are good" — general piety from Quentyn
The Old Blood of Volantis (ancient Valyrian families within the Black Wall)
Braavosi (descended from escaped slaves; do not trade in Slaver's Bay)
Ghiscari (New Ghis and Yunkai share Ghiscari heritage)
Valyrians (historical; built the Black Wall, the demon road)
Ibbenese (hairy; present at the Merchant's House)
Qartheen (pale-skinned; present at the Merchant's House)
Summer Islanders (coal-black, feathered cloaks; present at the Merchant's House)
Shadow-binders from Asshai (masked; present at the Merchant's House)
Lyseni (implied by the stop in Lys)
Westerosi (traders from Oldtown and King's Landing at the Merchant's House)
Cyvasse pieces (ivory and jade, sold in Volantis)
Iron chains and manacles (sold in Volantis)
Maester Kedry's book about the Free Cities
Slave tattoos (wheel, blue feathers mask, lightning bolt, coin, leopard's spots, skull, jug)
Maester Kedry's book about the Nine Free Cities (given to Gerris, unread)

mentions.raw_name: The Windblown marching song ("We are the Windblown… we'll kill the butcher king and fuck the dragon queen")
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: The war between Yunkai and Astapor (ongoing; sellsword companies being hired)
The corsair attack on the Meadowlark (off the Disputed Lands; killed Cletus, Will, Kedry, and twelve crewmen)
Triarch (one of three rulers of Volantis; chosen from Old Blood families; feet not allowed to touch the ground during year of service; rides an elephant)
Dwarf elephants (pull hathays through Old Volantis streets; the triarch's elephant is a grey-skinned behemoth in enameled armor)
Jeweled lizards, giant banded snakes, monkeys with striped tails and clever pink hands (sold by animal sellers at the river bend)
The Vulture King (historical reference — Gerris once led friends to find the old lair)
Dragon roads (Valyrian stone roadways; the demon road is the one running east from Volantis to Meereen)
Ships named: Adventure, Meadowlark, Melantine, Triarch's Daughter, Mermaid's Kiss, Bold Voyager, Dolphin, Seventh Son, Fawn, Goddess
